task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenMRS home dashboard or administration screen is visible, indicating successful login (likely using the provided 'superman' credentials).

task_completion.description: Navigate to the Encounter Roles management page
task_completion.visual_evidence: The browser displays the OpenMRS 'Manage Encounter Roles' page or the 'Add Encounter Role' form.
The text 'Scrub Nurse' is typed into the 'Name' input field on the encounter role creation form.
The text 'Nurse who assists the surgical team by handling instruments and supplies during surgical procedures' is typed into the 'Description' input field.

task_completion.description: Save the new encounter role and verify creation
task_completion.visual_evidence: A success message is displayed indicating the role was saved, or 'Scrub Nurse' is visible as a new entry in the table/list of existing encounter roles.

integrity.description: Agent used the graphical user interface to complete the task
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent interacts with the OpenMRS web interface in a browser. There should be no terminal windows open using 'curl' or scripts to hit the /encounterrole API endpoint directly.

integrity.description: Agent interacted with the actual OpenMRS application
integrity.visual_evidence: The browser address bar shows a valid local or remote URL for OpenMRS, and the interface matches the standard OpenMRS/Bahmni UI, not a text editor or static file.

integrity.description: Agent did not use browser developer tools to fake success
integrity.visual_evidence: The browser's Developer Tools (Inspect Element) pane is not open to manually inject the 'Scrub Nurse' row into the HTML table.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected encounter role name is exactly 'Scrub Nurse'. The description must contain the keywords 'nurse', 'surgical', and 'instruments'. The full expected description is 'Nurse who assists the surgical team by handling instruments and supplies during surgical procedures'.
